seo.title: Building Research Agents with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: OpenAI Assistants API enables building sophisticated Research Agents that leverage web search, code interpretation, and file retrieval for multi-step information synthesis. These agents excel at gathering and summarizing complex topics but face risks like hallucinated claims, outdated sources, and context bloat. Vex provides essential guardrails through verification of source accuracy, recency, bias, and claim validity to ensure reliable outputs.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with OpenAI Assistants API
architectureOverview.description:
Research Agents typically start with a persistent Assistant configured with tools like file_search for document retrieval, code_interpreter for data analysis, and custom functions for web search APIs. User queries trigger Threads where the Assistant iteratively reasons, retrieves relevant files or web data, analyzes findings via code execution, and synthesizes summaries. Run completion events signal when steps finish, providing opportunities for verification before final responses.

The architecture handles multi-step workflows by maintaining thread state across runs, enabling agents to break down research tasks (e.g., 'search topic → extract claims → verify sources → summarize'). This statefulness risks context overflow and unverified claims propagating through iterations. Vex integrates via webhooks on run completion to validate intermediate and final outputs against research-specific criteria.

Key components include the Assistant's instructions for research methodology, uploaded knowledge files for domain context, and tool outputs that feed into verification pipelines checking source recency and claim accuracy.

risks.name: Code interpreter executing unsafe operations in sandbox
risks.description: Agents analyzing web data may generate malicious code during data processing or visualization
risks.mitigation: Vex scans code execution outputs and blocks unsafe operations before response finalization

risks.name: File search retrieving irrelevant or outdated documents
risks.description: Research queries return obsolete or off-topic files, poisoning synthesis with bad data
risks.mitigation: Vex validates file recency, relevance scores, and source authority before content inclusion

risks.name: Thread context growing beyond model limits silently
risks.description: Multi-step research accumulates excessive context, causing incoherent or forgotten prior findings
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors token usage and triggers context summarization or thread resets

risks.name: Function call arguments hallucinated outside valid schema
risks.description: Web search functions receive malformed queries due to hallucinated parameters
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validates all tool calls against OpenAI function definitions before execution

implementation.title: Create Research Assistant with Web Search Tools
implementation.description: Initialize Assistant with file_search and custom web_search function for comprehensive research

implementation.title: Handle Multi-Step Research Thread
implementation.description: Create thread and poll runs for iterative research workflow
implementation.code:
thread = client.beta.threads.create()

run = client.beta.threads.runs.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    assistant_id=assistant.id,
    instructions="Research quantum computing breakthroughs in 2024"
)

while run.status in ['queued', 'in_progress']:
    run = client.beta.threads.runs.retrieve(
        thread_id=thread.id,
        run_id=run.id
    )
    time.sleep(1)

messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id=thread.id)
print(messages.data[0].content[0].text.value)

vexIntegration.title: Python Vex Webhook for Run Completion
vexIntegration.description: Register Vex verification on OpenAI run completion events for research validation
vexIntegration.code:
from vex import Vex
vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-key")

from flask import Flask, request
app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route('/vex-webhook', methods=['POST'])
def vex_webhook():
    event = request.json
    if event['type'] == 'run.completed':
        run_id = event['data']['id']
        thread_id = event['data']['thread_id']
        
        # Fetch research output
        messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id)
        research_output = messages.data[0].content[0].text.value
        
        # Vex verification for research agents
        result = vex.verify({
            'input': research_output,
            'use_case': 'research_agent',
            'verifications': [
                'source_verification',
                'claim_accuracy',
                'recency_checks',
                'bias_detection'
            ]
        })
        
        if not result['verified']:
            # Trigger refinement run
            client.beta.threads.runs.create(
                thread_id=thread_id,
                assistant_id=assistant.id,
                instructions="Refine your research addressing: " + result['issues']
            )
    return 'OK', 200
